title: Does Anno hate Rei
wikitext:
[]: Does Anno hate Rei?             Quote Post by Collossus » Mon Jun 03, 2013 2:18 pm I was reading some interviews, and I saw some things: "The truth is, I have no emotional attachment to Rei at all." Or: When told that the American audience favors Misato... ANNO: I'm surprised. In Japan, the overwhelming favorite is Rei. They can't handle strong women such as Misato and Asuka So, do you think he hates Rei?

[]:             Quote Post by Catamari » Mon Jun 03, 2013 2:26 pm I don't think he hates Rei, but I don't think she's a character he necessarily likes . Think of this way, I don't like Will Ferrell, but I don't hate the man. His lack of "emotional attachment" to Rei is, in my opinion, due to base personal preference.

[]:             Quote Post by CX1329 » Mon Jun 03, 2013 2:27 pm The conclusion that Anno hates Rei cannot possibly follow from those quotes. I have no emotional attachment to ham, but I do not hate ham. Likewise, hypothetically, I could say people like ham because they can't handle bacon, and that wouldn't mean I hate ham.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Mon Jun 03, 2013 2:44 pm He doesn't hate her. He just doesn't care about her and considers her expendable

[]:             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Mon Jun 03, 2013 11:51 pm Here's a quote from Anno regarding Rei that might prove insightful (this is from the Wiki, BTW, right here: [url]http://wiki.evageeks.org/Theory_and_Analysis:Rei_Ayanami_%28In-Depth%29[/url]) Anno understands the Japanese national attraction to characters like Rei as the product of a stunted imaginative landscape borne of Japan's defeat in the Second World War. Japan lost the war to the Americans. Since that time, the education we received is not one that creates adults. Even for us, people in their 40s, and for the generation older than me, in their 50s and 60s, there's no reasonable model of what an adult should be like.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Jun 04, 2013 5:07 am If Anno hated Rei, he wouldn't have included writing that character to begin with. He only dropped the character for a while after Episode 6 because he had no further ideas for progressing the character beyond that point.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Wed Jun 05, 2013 5:03 am I certainly don't think he hates Rei. What I think he does hate, or at least I think mildly annoys him, is some fans's reaction to her. He originally envisaged her as superficially attractive and a sympathetic character, but also somewhat inhuman and deeply unsettling. Of course, quite a few people misinterpreted her as some kind of perfect girlfriend, as opposed to the somewhat unnerving, creepy individual she was supposed to be. Myself, I view her as Anno intended; I like Rei, and I think she's a sympathetic character, but I did find her very unsettling.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Jun 06, 2013 7:41 pm I think it's more accurate to say that he simply has a weird relationship with writing her, as these artist types sometimes do. At times, he seems weirdly detached (which is perhaps parallel to Rei's own feelings being a bit far from her surface, because there's this irregular flow), there was one time where some kid asked him what Rei likes, and he couldn't answer - but he may just generally be the type who doesn't consider the characters beyond what happens on screen and works from scene to scene. Or that may be because she (like Kaworu) is from the "unconcious" parts of him, as he himself said. The Rebuild CR has him state that he is "unaware" when it comes to writing Rei, that he doesn't allways quite understand why he makes her do this or that; Allegedly, "poka poka" was her own idea XD There's this statement about him feeling he'd written himself into a corner by ep 6, but he says it's because that point already had a sucessfull communication between Shinji & Rei transpiring; Indeed, there's this quote: EoE wrote: "But who are you within my heart?" "We are the hope that people can understand each other." "And the words I love you."

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Fri Jun 07, 2013 1:31 pm Kendrix wrote: In any case, he seems to have since worked up what she likes: Apparently Books. And Shinji's home-cooking.

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Mon Jun 10, 2013 1:32 am Squigsquasher wrote: (Great, now I can't get the image of Rei pretending to be Swedish Chef out of my head...)

[]:             Quote Post by Katana » Mon Jun 10, 2013 1:49 am Squigsquasher wrote: Who wouldn't like Shinji's home cooking? (Great, now I can't get the image of Rei pretending to be Swedish Chef out of my head...)

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Mon Jun 10, 2013 3:00 pm I do sometimes think that Anno hates Rei's fans. Whenever I see that quote about being a nation of children after WW2 it makes me wonder if he romatisizes Imperial Japan. And lets face it Imperial Japan were assholes.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Mon Jun 10, 2013 3:32 pm Darkwing wrote: Whenever I see that quote about being a nation of children after WW2 it makes me wonder if he romatisizes Imperial Japan. And lets face it Imperial Japan were assholes.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Mon Jun 10, 2013 4:05 pm LeoXiao wrote: The Germans have a similar problem where they tend to think that just because Nazis everything their country did before 1945 was just awful. But they have it better because at least they owned up to their crimes whereas the Japanese are just like "yeah let's not go there".

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Mon Jun 10, 2013 4:30 pm Ah yes, the good doctor Shiro Ishii. They invited him to the States and he lived somewhere back east until he died of cancer, IIRC.

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Mon Jun 10, 2013 5:32 pm Yeah, sadly the US let those bastards from Unit 731 off. But the thing that bugs me is that it sometimes seems like Anno is romatisizing the military culture of Imperial Japan. Maybe I'm reading too much into it. But as I understand it Japan doesn't really want to ever admit they did these aweful things in WW2. And those that forget the past are doomed to repeat it. Again I might just be reading too much into it.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Mon Jun 10, 2013 5:47 pm Darkwing wrote: Yeah, sadly the US let those bastards from Unit 731 off. But the thing that bugs me is that it sometimes seems like Anno is romatisizing the military culture of Imperial Japan. Maybe I'm reading too much into it. But as I understand it Japan doesn't really want to ever admit they did these aweful things in WW2. And those that forget the past are doomed to repeat it. Again I might just be reading too much into it.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jun 11, 2013 4:27 am TOPIC, BITCHES!

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Tue Jun 11, 2013 4:32 am Rei did try to kamikaze herself on two occasions. The first time she used a nuke and it didn't work. From the perspective of any patriotic Japanese, this is utterly shameful. The second time she succeeded in self-destruction, only to be brought back to life. Again, the worst dishonour one could face. Therefore, Anno the Imperial Japanese romanticist hates Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Thu Jun 13, 2013 9:51 am I don't think he hates her as so much he finds the common ways fans relate to her character distasteful. I would definitely say he hates how Rei has become a sex symbol for the otaku generation.
summary:
[]: Does Anno hate Rei?             Quote Post by Collossus » Mon Jun 03, 2013 2:18 pm I was reading some interviews, and I saw some things: "The truth is, I have no emotional attachment to Rei at all." Or: When told that the American audience favors Misato... ANNO: I'm surprised. In Japan, the overwhelming favorite is Rei. They can't handle strong women such as Misato and Asuka So, do you think he hates Rei?

[]:  &n
